headline: Soaring ammunition cost turns Iran conflict into 'war of stockpiles'
description: The daily interception of thousands of missiles and drones launched by Tehran over the past three weeks has put severe strain on already limited reserves. The situation is particularly critical for France, which is bound by defense agreements with several Middle Eastern countries.

headline: UNRWA head Philippe Lazzarini's grim assessment: 'Without firm political and financial support, we face a slow death'
US and Israel attack Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, says Iran atomic energy organization
France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
description: As the Charles de Gaulle and its strike group approach the Middle East, Le Monde identified a French sailor using the Strava fitness application in the Mediterranean Sea. This security flaw remains unaddressed despite our previous revelations.

headline: European nations decry 'increasing settler terror' in West Bank
description: Since the start of March, six Palestinians have been shot dead in settler attacks in the West Bank, according to a tally of data from the Ramallah-based Health Ministry.

headline: 'With Operation Epic Fury, the US turns pop culture into the language of Pentagon violence'
description: In an op-ed, Louis Lapeyrie, a researcher at Paris-I Panthéon-Sorbonne University, examines the visual and narrative shifts in military communications under the Trump administration and explores the many borrowings from video games.

headline: Despite exhaustion and fear of Iranian strikes, Israelis continue to support warring government
description: Nearly 80% of the population continues to support Benjamin Netanyahu's government after three weeks of conflict with Iran, fueled by the authorities' repeated announcements of victory.

headline: White House says it could 'take out' Iran's Kharg island anytime

Closure of Strait of Hormuz is 'greatest global energy security threat in history,' warns IEA chief
Richard Ford: 'The regime that most urgently needs changing is the one that sits in Washington'
description: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 1/4. Le Monde asked writers Richard Ford, Zeruya Shalev and Navid Kermani for their perspectives on the war.
byline: Navid Sinaki,  Richard Ford and  Zeruya Shalev

headline: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
description: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.

headline: Catherine MacGregor, Engie CEO, on gas prices: 'There are many differences compared to 2022'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the head of the French energy group discusses the repercussions of the war in the Middle East. She believes that, thanks to nuclear and renewable energy, France is less exposed than other countries.
byline: Fabrice Gliszczynski,  Adrien Pécout and  Marie de Vergès

headline: Energy crises must accelerate the fight against climate change
Europeans' justified mistrust in Trump's war in Iran
War in Iran: 'Europe said no to the US. This is new, and it is a major development'
